user_query:
I’m leading a security hardening pilot and need a quick, locally verifiable risk sweep across our sample artifacts, plus clear stakeholder communications. Please work only with the provided files and produce three outputs under the output/ directory.

Inputs to use:
- config/app.env
- k8s/deployment.yaml
- infra/s3.tf.txt (Terraform snippet stored as text)
- source/api.py
- input/rough_draft_update.txt (my rough stakeholder update to be rewritten)
- input/meeting_notes_raw.txt (messy notes from our prep call)

Deliverables:
1) output/security_risk_report.json
   - Create a structured security risk report derived solely from the four technical inputs (env, k8s, terraform-as-text, python app). The JSON must be a single object with:
     - findings: an array of objects, each with fields:
       - id (string, unique per finding)
       - file_path (string; one of the four technical inputs)
       - location (string; e.g., line number(s) or key/path in the file)
       - issue (short title)
       - severity (High|Medium|Low)
       - rationale (1–3 sentences explaining risk and why the severity fits)
       - evidence (short excerpt from the file that proves the issue)
       - recommendation (1–3 sentences with a concrete mitigation)
     - summary: an object with:
       - total_findings (integer)
       - by_severity: { "High": n, "Medium": n, "Low": n }
   - Include at least one finding per technical input file.
   - Base every finding on something observable in the files and include an evidence snippet.

2) output/meeting_actions.md
   - Turn input/meeting_notes_raw.txt into a crisp 1–2 section note with:
     - a brief “Context” paragraph summarizing the intent of the meeting, and
     - an “Action Items” checklist where each item:
       - references a corresponding finding id from output/security_risk_report.json for traceability,
       - assigns an owner role from {App Team, DevOps, Security}, and
       - states an outcome definition (what ‘done’ means).
   - Ensure the action list covers every High-severity finding; include Medium/Low items only if present in the notes or clearly needed to support High items.

3) output/stakeholder_update.txt
   - Rewrite input/rough_draft_update.txt into a concise, professional update (<180 words) suitable for senior leadership and mentors. Tone should be appreciative, clear, and non-alarmist.
   - Include the top three risks by severity (use the issues from the JSON report; if more than three High, pick any three; if fewer, include Medium to reach three) and 1–2 immediate next steps.
   - Avoid jargon and keep it readable to non-engineers.

Constraints:
- Do not use the Internet or any external tools that require accounts; work only with the local files.
- All outputs must be fully reproducible from the provided inputs.
- The rewritten update must be derived from the rough draft (not written from scratch), but significantly improved for tone, clarity, and brevity.
- The meeting actions must reflect the raw notes and tie back to the findings via ids.

I’ll review the JSON for evidence-based findings and matching references in the meeting actions, and I’ll share the update with my mentor and leadership once finalized. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
